rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible ports and data types present in the AADL model.
Component type contracts must not use implementation-specific subcomponent paths or ports.
AGREE annexes should be placed in the appropriate AADL scope to ensure valid expression and reference resolution.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should match the data types of visible AADL features.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
